concept: Meditate / Practice Focused Awareness / Cultivate Mind
description: To engage in deliberate practice of focused awareness—sitting with attention anchored, watching the mind, cultivating stillness and insight. 'Phirena' names the disciplined practice that develops over time, not a single moment of calm but the ongoing work of training attention.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'phi' /ɸi/, breath entering gently as practice begins with breathing. The 'i' focuses inward, narrow and precise. Then 're' reflects, awareness turning to observe itself. Finally 'na' settles with nasal grounding, the stable base of established practice.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an intransitive verb: 'mia phirena.' (I meditate.) Habitual practice takes the aspect particle 'ro': 'mia ro phirena.' (I meditate regularly.) Can take temporal phrases: 'mia sui kelua phirena.' (I meditate during the morning.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Meditation is the core practice—training the mind through sustained attention, developing insight into the nature of experience.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Meditation cultivates the inner stillness from which peaceful speech arises. The practice precedes and enables right communication.
